D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

White-Point Adaptation (CAT02)

move a screen from D65 to D50 and a chromatic adaptation transform decides which way every colour drifts

Changing a display's white point shifts every colour. A chromatic adaptation transform (CAT) does this the way the eye does: convert to cone-like responses, rescale each cone by the ratio of destination to source white, convert back. CAT02 is the matrix used inside CIECAM; Bradford and von Kries are alternatives.

Pick a direction and a transform, then sweep the degree of adaptation D from 0 (no change) to full. Watch the patch grid warm or cool, and compare how CAT02, Bradford, and plain von Kries push the same colours differently.
